_meta.description: Tool-access matrix and scheduling rules for the AI-agent office's external creative-tool usage (NotebookLM, Stitch, Base44, Google AI Studio). None of these tools have an unattended API — agents DECIDE and QUEUE tool-tasks (asset-pipeline board entry + asset-task issue spec), a human executes the work in the real tool, and the returned asset comes back into the repo for agent testing/optimization. This config is read by agent-runner.js's tool_task_window block (see daily-schedule.json) to decide, each Sun-Thu sim day, which agent (if any) may open a new tool-task.
_meta.human_in_the_loop: Agents never call these tools programmatically. 'Opening a tool-task' means: append a row to agents/reports/asset-pipeline/board.json (status: queued) and write an asset-task spec file under agents/reports/asset-pipeline/issues/. A human picks it up, does the work in the real tool, and updates the board row to 'returned' with a path/link to the asset. Agents then move it through tested -> optimized -> implemented.

agent_roles.6: primary — builds and extends the project's databases / knowledge centers

notebooklm.goal: The Claude app's AI Search should eventually answer almost exclusively from QA-built NotebookLM knowledge centers (see standing project #1 in asset-pipeline board).

agent_roles.9: joint sessions with Architect — large/high-value tool & UI generation
agent_roles.10: joint sessions with Designer — large/high-value tool & UI generation

stitch.purpose: Generate large, high-value tools/projects (e.g. the Archives app, Designer tooling suite components).

agent_roles.any_admin: may use for small admin-tier product needs
agent_roles.9: most frequent — design tooling suite products
agent_roles.10: most frequent — org products, often paired with Designer

agent_roles.9: joint or independent — generative design/content tooling
agent_roles.10: joint or independent — generative architecture/content tooling

scheduling_rules.description: Applied at the daily 'tool_task_window' block (full_day_schedule, Sun-Thu only — see daily-schedule.json).
general: Use ONLY the four listed tools for content/product generation. Routine cases and information-gathering use the regular models (Gemini for sim, Claude for AI Search) — never burn creative-tool budget on routine cases.
Default product type is a LIGHTWEIGHT app/document unless the spec explicitly calls for something heavier (e.g. Stitch's 'large, high-value' remit).
Stagger usage: no two agents may use the SAME tool in the SAME day's tool_task_window.
Joint sessions (Stitch always; Base44/Google AI Studio when both Designer and Architect opt in) get priority booking over solo sessions for the same tool/day.
An agent may only open a new tool-task if its current open (queued or in-progress) board.json items for that tool are below max_concurrent_per_agent_per_tool.
scheduling_rules.eligibility_check: At each Sun-Thu sim-day start, for the day's assigned tool (per weekly_rotation), check the assigned agent(s)' open board.json items for that tool. If below the concurrency limit AND a standing project (asset-pipeline board) has a 'queued'-eligible next step, open one new tool-task (board row + asset-task spec file). Otherwise, skip silently (no API cost either way — this is a D1/file read + conditional file write, not a Gemini/Claude call).

weekly_rotation.description: 5-day Sun-Thu rotation, one tool-task slot per day, staggered across tools and agents so no two agents compete for the same tool on the same day.
